text.title: Research at Scale Tools, Services, and Best Practices
text.summary: CLI tools for transcription and analysis, platform service landscape, and operational best practices for large-scale research pipelines.

list.item: whisperx: forced-aligned transcription with diarization, faster than vanilla Whisper
pyannote-audio: speaker diarization when WhisperX is insufficient
ffmpeg: slice session recordings into per-task clips for AI scoring
yt-dlp: pull session videos from CDN URLs given by panel platforms
jq / duckdb: query coded JSONL transcripts and platform exports
pandas + sentence-transformers: embed and cluster open-ended survey responses
bertopic: topic modeling on large response corpora
dvc / lakefs: version control for transcripts + codebooks (audit trail)
presidio: PII redaction before sending to external LLMs

rule.statement: Gate on Whisper confidence score >= 0.6 per transcript before coding — low-quality audio produces confident-but-wrong text that the coder cannot distinguish from accurate transcription.
rule.rationale: Downstream coding errors from bad transcription are undetectable at the finding layer. The confidence gate catches the problem at the source.

list.item: UserTesting (enterprise): REST API + webhook; largest panel, AI Insights add-on
Userlytics (mid+): REST API; 2M+ panel, AI analytics, EU-friendly
Maze (self-serve+): API + Zapier; bias detection, dynamic follow-ups
dscout (enterprise): API; mobile ethnography and longitudinal diaries
Dovetail (all tiers): REST + GraphQL; repository + AI tagging, codebook host
Sprig (mid): API + JS SDK; in-product micro-surveys at session scale
Hotjar / FullStory (mid): API; session replay + heatmaps for behavior layer
Rally / User Interviews (mid): API; recruitment and scheduling automation

rule.statement: Cache full transcripts in the LLM context using prompt caching when coding hundreds of quotes against the same codebook — the savings are substantial at scale.
rule.rationale: Repeated codebook context is identical across all transcript coding calls. Prompt caching eliminates the redundant token cost of loading the codebook on every call.

rule.statement: Publish disconfirming quotes alongside confirming ones — outliers are signal, not noise.
rule.rationale: LLM summarization compresses outliers. The most valuable insights often come from the user who had the unexpected reaction. Requiring outlier inclusion in the quote bank prevents systematic suppression.

rule.statement: Stratify panels by behavior (active/lapsed/new), not just demographics — demographic-only sampling hides usage truth.
rule.rationale: Demographic similarity does not predict usage similarity. Active and churned users of the same age have opposite perspectives on the product's value.

rule.statement: Triangulate every finding with AI sentiment + behavioral analytics + verbatim quote — never report from one source alone.
rule.rationale: Single-source findings have low reliability at scale. Three-source triangulation with confidence tiers (high >= 3 types, medium = 2, low = 1) enables stakeholders to calibrate trust.

rule.statement: Add a "draft/published" gate on every finding — dashboards auto-update faster than humans can vet.
rule.rationale: Unvetted AI findings reaching a product meeting without a human review can redirect roadmap decisions based on hallucinated patterns.
